mnemonics: I Am Tiny Tiger's Incredible Node Traverses Securely
keyPoints: Insertion: Insert a node at the right position in a binary search tree.
Traversal: Inorder, preorder, postorder traversals. Inorder: Left-Root-Right, Preorder: Root-Left-Right, Postorder: Left-Right-Root.
Searching: Find a node with a given key in a binary search tree by traversing the tree.
Copying a Tree: Make a copy of a binary tree by traversing the tree and creating a new node at each visited position.
Binary Search Trees: A binary tree where for each node, all elements in the left subtree are less than the node, and all elements in the right subtree are greater than the node.
Insertion in Binary Search Trees: Insert a node with a given key in a binary search tree by traversing the tree and maintaining the binary search tree property.
Searching in Binary Search Trees: Find a node with a given key in a binary search tree by traversing the tree and maintaining the binary search tree property.
Find Maximum in Binary Search Trees: Find the maximum element in a binary search tree by traversing the tree from left to right.
Find Minimum in Binary Search Trees: Find the minimum element in a binary search tree by traversing the tree from left to right.
